summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the Dec 26 1969 Dallas show, highlighted by a breathtaking acoustic set and a soaring Dark Star.
review: The recording is an excellent sound‑board source – crisp, silky in the lows and crystal clear in the highs – that lets listeners hear every nuance of this transitional 1969 performance.  The first (acoustic) half dazzles with “All Around This World,” “Little Sadie,” “Long Black Limo” and a flawless “Uncle John’s Band,” while the electric set delivers a luminous Dark Star, perfect China>Rider flow and an inspired organ‑driven second half.  Overall both the music and the tape quality are consistently praised by fans.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen five‑star reviews, all praising both performance and sound; only minor comments about EQ tweaks or a badly cut New Speedway Boogie keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the tape as crisp, silky‑smooth in the lows and crystal clear in the highs; some suggest EQ tweaks but overall the soundboard capture is superb with no noticeable distortion or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: An intimate acoustic set transitions smoothly into the electric half via Uncle John’s Band; the second set builds momentum with a perfect China>Rider segue and culminates in an otherworldly Dark Star.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his bright Fender Strat tone, inventive ideas on Dark Star and overall stellar guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted as especially strong this night; bass tone (Alembic‑modified Gibson EB‑0) noted positively.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal harmonies described as spot‑on throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s B‑3 organ work called inspired and a key driver of the second set energy.
